report_name: Phishing Attack on Bozeman School District Exposes SSNs of Over 2,600 Staff

threat_actors.name: Unauthorized Party
threat_actors.description: The unidentified threat actor responsible for the phishing attack against Bozeman School District #7, gaining access via compromised credentials and exfiltrating staff PII between February and March 2026.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for financial gain or identity fraud.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Used as the likely initial access vector that led to credential compromise, allowing an unauthorized party to gain access to the school district's network.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attacker used legitimate, stolen credentials to access and move within the network, remaining undetected for over a month.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: The attacker collected sensitive data, including the names and Social Security numbers of 2,617 staff members, from the compromised systems.

identities.name: Bozeman School District #7
identities.description: A school district in Montana that was the victim of a phishing attack, resulting in a data breach of current and former staff members' PII.

identities.name: Bozeman School District #7 Staff
identities.description: A class of 2,617 current and former staff members whose names and Social Security numbers were compromised in the data breach.

indicators.name: Anomalous Account Login Behavior
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Account Login Behavior
id: 9c5b1a3e-8d7f-4c6b-a5e4-3d2c1b0a9f8e
status: experimental
description: Detects user account login behavior that deviates from established baselines, such as logins from unusual geographic locations or at odd hours. This is a conceptual rule representing UEBA capabilities.
references:
  - https://www.almeidalawgroup.com/data-breach-news/bozeman-school-district-7-data-breach-investigation/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: ueba
  service: alert
detection:
  selection:
    RuleName|contains:
      - 'Anomalous Login Location'
      - 'Impossible Travel'
      - 'Login at Unusual Time'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate user activity that deviates from the norm, such as travel or working late.
indicators.description: Detects account logins from unusual geographic locations or at odd hours for staff accounts, a common indicator of compromised credentials as mentioned in the report's hunting hints.

indicators.name: Potential Data Staging Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Data Staging Activity
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects unusually large data transfers from internal file servers (e.g., HR, finance) to a single non-administrative workstation, which could indicate an attacker staging data for exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.almeidalawgroup.com/data-breach-news/bozeman-school-district-7-data-breach-investigation/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t1074.001
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_traffic
detection:
  selection:
    SourceIp:
      - '10.10.1.5' # Placeholder for HR/Finance servers
      - '10.10.1.6'
    BytesOut|gt: 100000000 # Example threshold: 100MB
  filter:
    DestinationIp:
      - '10.20.0.0/16' # Placeholder for admin/backup subnets
  condition: selection and not filter
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate large file transfers for administrative or backup purposes.
indicators.description: Detects large data transfers from internal file servers to a single internal workstation, which could indicate an attacker staging data for exfiltration, as suggested by the report's hunting hints.
